title: Taming the 3D Wilson-Fisher Fixed Point via Nonlocal Effective Action
authors: Hyeon Jung Kim, Seung-Jong Yoo, Jinmo Bok, Lemuel John Sese, Semin Park, Ki-Seok Kim
abstract: We present a novel Renormalization Group (RG) framework based on a nonlocal effective action ansatz to tame the strong coupling dynamics of the three-dimensional relativistic $φ^{4}$ theory. By implementing a Hubbard-Stratonovich transformation, we decouple the quartic interaction into a system of the primary field $φ$ and an auxiliary field $\varphi \sim φ^2$. Rather than freezing the intermediate scaling dimensions, the nonlocality of our effective action allows both exponents $Δ_φ$ and $Δ_{\varphi}$ to act as fully independent, unconstrained dynamical variables.This nonlocal propagator framework plays a critical role in the RG flow: evaluating self-energies and vertex fluctuations up to the three-loop order, the nonlocality drives precise structural cross-cancellations among multi-loop fluctuations near the Gaussian limit. Solving the resulting closed two-variable master equations isolates a robust, non-trivial physical fixed point at $Δ_φ^{*} \approx 0.981$ and $Δ_{\varphi}^{*} \approx 0.415$. These dynamic exponents yield a kinematic anomalous dimension $η_φ \approx 0.038$, an energy operator dimension $Δ_{φ^2} \approx 1.417$, and-via mass deformation-a thermal correlation length exponent $ν\approx 0.6317$, demonstrating exceptional quantitative agreement with high-precision Quantum Monte Carlo (QMC) and conformal bootstrap benchmarks. Our results rigorously confirm that unfreezing the nonlocal degrees of freedom successfully eliminates the systematic truncation errors inherent to conventional local ansatz treatments, simultaneously resolving both the static scaling and thermodynamic flows of the Wilson-Fisher universality class.
summary: The authors propose a new Renormalization Group framework that uses a nonlocal effective action to solve the 3D Wilson-Fisher fixed point problem. By treating the scaling dimensions of both primary and auxiliary fields as independent variables, they eliminate systematic truncation errors. This approach yields critical exponents that are highly accurate when compared to established numerical benchmarks.
main_problem: The difficulty of accurately characterizing the 3D Wilson-Fisher quantum critical point due to severe truncation errors and unphysical fixed-point divergence in standard non-perturbative Renormalization Group frameworks.
main_result: The identification of a robust, non-trivial physical fixed point with critical exponents (eta ~ 0.038, nu ~ 0.6317) that show exceptional agreement with Quantum Monte Carlo and conformal bootstrap benchmarks.
method: A multi-loop Renormalization Group approach using a nonlocal effective action ansatz and a two-variable master equation system evaluated up to three-loop order.
model_or_system: A 3D relativistic phi^4 theory (Wilson-Fisher universality class) where the quartic interaction is decoupled via a Hubbard-Stratonovich transformation into a primary field and an auxiliary field.
key_observables: Kinematic anomalous dimension (eta_phi), energy operator dimension (Delta_phi^2), and thermal correlation length exponent (nu).
important_parameters: Scaling dimensions (Delta_phi and Delta_phi_tilde) treated as independent dynamical variables, and the dimensionless coupling (g_tilde^2).
main_assumptions: The framework assumes a scale-invariant action at the critical point and utilizes a three-loop truncation of the effective action.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the intersection of the two independent field flow equations (gamma_phi = 0 and gamma_phi_tilde = 0) on the scaling dimension plane, marking the stable physical root.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of truncation errors in RG, presents a nonlocal effective action ansatz using Hubbard-Stratonovich transformation, details the multi-loop diagrammatic calculations, demonstrates the numerical solution of the two-variable master equations, and validates the results against benchmarked data.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. The intersection of the independent field flow equa- tions γϕ = 0 and γφ = 0 on the (∆ϕ, ∆φ) plane. The un- constrained physical non-trivial root cleanly stabilizes at the crossing point (0.981, 0.415), isolating the true local Wilson- Fisher critical coordinates without requiring any fine-tuned kinematic matching constraints.
